Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: The weight is almost over. The first televised face-off between Hillary Clinton and Donald

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: Trump will get underway in less than four hours from now. It's expected to be the most watched

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: presidential debate in history and could even rival ratings seen during the Super Bowl.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: Yunshin reports.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: Their first historic base-off is almost here. Democratic presidential candidate Hillary Clinton and

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: a Republican rival Donald Trump are gearing up for their first debate on Monday night in one of

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: this century's most anticipated TV events. Moderated by NBC News anchor Lester Holt,

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: it's a 90-minute session starting at 9 p.m. at New York's Hofstra University.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: Clinton and Trump are expected to cover topics including foreign policy and national security issues.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: Clinton has three decades of experience in politics and a long history of high-profile debates.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: Last Wednesday, she put a pause on campaigning to prepare for the debate.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: And Donald Trump, who vanquished nearly a dozen opponents in the Republican debates,

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: has also been getting ready while on the campaign trail.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: It's reported that he's been studying Clinton's past debates to find flaws

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: in her stances on national issues, with the help of Steve Venon, the CEO of the Trump campaign,

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: and others on his team. Just six weeks ahead of election day, the polls show the two candidates

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: neck and neck. A poll released on Sunday by ABC News on the Washington Post showed voters are

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: split, with 46% for Clinton and 44% for Trump, well within the polls four and a half percentage point

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: margin of error. Described as debate of the century, Monday night's showdown is expected to draw more

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: than 100 million viewers in the States alone. And with the rest of the world eyeing this crucial

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: point in the US election, it'll be streamed live online through platforms like YouTube.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: Ian Shit, Arirang News.
